title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: Tel Aviv, Israel / New York (Global, hybrid)
description: Finout is a cloud cost management B2B SaaS company (trusted by The New York Times, Wiz, Elastic, Lyft) backed by $85M+ and 100+ people across Tel Aviv and New York. Reporting to the CRO, the Head of Revenue Operations builds and scales the operational backbone of the GTM engine. Requires 7+ years in RevOps/Sales Ops/Strategy in high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and a builder's mentality collaborating across globally distributed teams including the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment across all dimensions: 15+ years exceeds the 7+ requirement, B2B SaaS fintech background matches perfectly, Head of RevOps role is target position, and scaling experience aligns with $30M+ ARR growth stage. Tel Aviv HQ with global hybrid setup matches location preferences exactly.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's impressive $85M+ raise and growth trajectory in cloud cost management—a space that demands sophisticated RevOps infrastructure to scale GTM across distributed teams. With your 15+ years building and scaling revenue operations engines in high-growth B2B SaaS, particularly in fintech, you seem ideally positioned to build the operational backbone Finout needs as it scales beyond its current stage. The Head of Revenue Operations role reporting to the CRO would be a natural extension of your expertise, especially given the Tel Aviv-New York dual-hub structure.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: As RevOps Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations at Gong, you accelerate revenue growth through data-driven investment strategy, scalable territory design, and consultative partnership with business leaders. Responsibilities include territory mapping in Fullcast/Salesforce, capacity modeling, and market expansion projects. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue Operations or Sales Strategy, deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models and segmentation, strong analytical skills with Excel/SQL/BI tools (Tableau, Retool), and proficiency with Salesforce and territory management tools like Fullcast, Pigment, or Anaplan.
score_reason: Excellent match: 15+ years B2B SaaS RevOps experience directly aligns with territory strategy, Salesforce expertise, and data-driven GTM focus. Gong.io's AI-native platform and revenue acceleration mission match Omri's strong fit profile perfectly.
ai_opener: Omri, your deep RevOps background scaling B2B SaaS GTM caught my attention for Gong's Territory Strategy & Operations role. With your Salesforce mastery and pipeline forecasting expertise, you'd immediately impact how Gong optimizes territory design and capacity modeling across their expansion. I'd love to discuss how your experience driving data-driven revenue strategy could accelerate Gong's next growth phase.

title: RevOps Systems Manager
description: Actabl is a hospitality-tech SaaS company serving 10,000+ properties worldwide. The RevOps Systems Manager is responsible for administration, optimization, and strategic oversight of Salesforce and other RevOps-owned systems, ensuring scalability and security of the CRM and integrated tools. Requires Salesforce Advanced Administrator and App Builder certifications, 5+ years of hands-on Salesforce administration managing complex orgs independently, experience integrating tools like Outreach, Gong, PandaDoc, ZoomInfo, and proficiency in SOQL. Strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills are essential.
score_reason: Strong Salesforce expertise and RevOps systems background align well with core requirements, but this is a specialized admin/technical role rather than the strategic leadership positions Omri typically targets. The hospitality-tech vertical is outside his core B2B SaaS/Fintech/AI experience, and the heavy certification/SOQL focus suggests a more IC-heavy position than his seniority level typically pursues.
ai_opener: Hi there – I came across Actabl's RevOps Systems Manager role and your scale (10,000+ properties) is impressive. With 15+ years building GTM infrastructure across B2B SaaS, I've architected complex Salesforce orgs, integrated the exact stack you mentioned (Outreach, Gong, PandaDoc), and managed RevOps at scale. While my background leans toward strategic revenue operations leadership, I'm curious if there's appetite to discuss how someone with my systems depth could help you evolve this role toward broader RevOps strategy as you scale.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Nooks is the AI Sales Assistant Platform (ASAP) that automates busywork so reps can focus on selling, loved by teams at HubSpot, Rippling, and hundreds more. Backed by $70M+ from top VCs including Kleiner Perkins, the company has grown ARR 4x then 3x. As Nooks' first GTM Engineer, you'll serve as the technical co-pilot for the Sales Development organization, owning setup, optimization, and enablement within the Nooks platform. The role sits at the intersection of sales technology, automation, and enablement for a fast-scaling AI sales-tech company.
score_reason: Excellent alignment: GTM Engineer role directly matches Omri's RevOps/GTM expertise, AI-native platform fits his strong interest profile, and the sales technology/automation/enablement focus is core to senior RevOps work. Remote location and well-funded growth-stage company are perfect fits.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I came across Nooks and was impressed by the 4x→3x ARR trajectory and the customer logos (HubSpot, Rippling). As a senior RevOps executive with 15+ years in B2B SaaS, I've spent my career optimizing exactly what Nooks automates—sales busywork and pipeline visibility. The GTM Engineer role caught my attention because it sits at the intersection of sales tech, automation, and enablement—the exact space where I've driven the most revenue impact. I'd love to explore how my experience scaling GTM operations could help Nooks accelerate adoption within your SDR/AE organizations.

title: GTM Engineer
description: FacilityOS is a B2B SaaS company helping mid-to-large enterprises stay safe, compliant, and operationally sharp through its facility management platform. As GTM Engineer, you'll help build the company's first AI Workforce — a collection of intelligent agents helping Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success work smarter. You'll build, configure, and continuously improve these agents, working at the intersection of data, automation, and human workflows, designing how the AI systems connect, run, learn, and improve over time across the GTM motion.
score_reason: Strong alignment with Omri's GTM expertise and AI-native systems thinking. FacilityOS's focus on building intelligent agents for Sales/Marketing/CS workflows directly matches RevOps skill set around process automation, data flows, and cross-functional enablement. Remote role in target geography removes friction.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years optimizing GTM motion in B2B SaaS, and what caught my attention about FacilityOS is your bet on AI Workforce for Sales, Marketing, and CS. Building intelligent agents that learn across your revenue org—designing how data flows, automation triggers, and human workflows integrate—is exactly the kind of systems thinking that separates high-performing GTM operations from the rest. I'd be interested in exploring how to accelerate that vision.

title: GTM Engineer
description: This is a foundational GTM role where you'll own the systems and signals behind how the company identifies, segments, and reaches its ICP, stitching BDR, marketing, and sales ops into one streamlined operation and partnering with AEs and the Head of Marketing to turn signals into pipeline. Requires 5+ years in a rev ops, marketing ops, or sales ops role at an enterprise SaaS company, experience running account-based motions, fluency in HubSpot, Clay, and Apollo, genuine AI fluency (prompting, building small agents), and a systems-building, bias-to-action mindset.
score_reason: Exceptional match on experience level, core competencies (RevOps, HubSpot, systems thinking, pipeline signals), and AI-native GTM sophistication. Remote location and GTM Engineer title align perfectly with Omri's senior background and target roles.
ai_opener: Gradient Labs is building exactly the kind of AI-native GTM infrastructure that senior operators are hungry to architect—I've spent 15+ years stitching together RevOps, marketing ops, and sales ops into signal-driven machines, and your vision of BDR/marketing/sales ops integration through HubSpot and Clay resonates deeply. I've built account-based motions at scale and genuinely use AI for automation and insights, not just hype—curious if you're still filling this GTM Engineer role.

title: Head of Growth Marketing
description: Flex is hiring a Head of Growth Marketing to scale acquisition, activation, and monetization. Requires 6–10+ years of experience in growth, digital, or performance marketing, ideally in FinTech or B2B SaaS, plus 3+ years of people management building high-performing teams. Candidates should have proven success scaling organizations from $50M to $200M+ ARR, deep expertise in SEO, paid acquisition, website optimization, and demand generation, fluency in intent-based targeting and predictive analytics, and excellent copywriting and storytelling skills.
score_reason: Strong alignment on FinTech/B2B SaaS experience, scale expertise ($50M-$200M+ ARR), and people leadership. However, this is a pure growth marketing role emphasizing paid acquisition, SEO, copywriting, and demand gen—areas outside Omri's core RevOps/GTM operations wheelhouse. Better suited for a Growth Marketing Manager than a senior RevOps executive.
ai_opener: Hi Flex team—I've scaled GTM operations and pipeline forecasting across multiple $50M-$200M+ ARR FinTech/SaaS companies, and I'm impressed by your focus on intent-driven acquisition and predictive analytics. While my expertise is deeply rooted in RevOps and GTM infrastructure, I'd love to explore how operational rigor in demand capture and monetization metrics could complement your growth marketing vision.

title: Senior Director, Marketing Operations
description: Reporting to WalkMe's COO, this senior leadership role owns the Marketing Operations team and optimizes WalkMe's AI-first Marketing Operations function. You'll architect the global marketing operating model, track KPIs, manage stakeholders across the SAP ecosystem (including GDPR/CCPA compliance and multi-product attribution), and mentor a team of operations professionals. Requires 12+ years of progressive experience in Marketing Operations, RevOps, or a related GTM role within B2B SaaS, plus 5+ years of direct people leadership scaling high-performing teams, and excellent stakeholder management skills.
score_reason: Strong alignment on seniority, B2B SaaS expertise, and GTM operations architecture. Primary gap: role is Marketing Operations–focused rather than broader RevOps, and requires 5+ years people leadership which isn't confirmed in profile.
ai_opener: Omri, I noticed WalkMe is building out an AI-first Marketing Operations function under their COO—a role that demands exactly the kind of GTM systems thinking and stakeholder orchestration you've mastered across pipeline forecasting and RevOps ecosystems. Given your 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS operations, you'd bring a unique lens on multi-product attribution and cross-functional modeling that most pure marketing ops leaders miss.

title: Head of Sales (Mid-Market B2B)
description: Reporting to the Chief Growth Officer, the Head of Sales leads the full sales organization (5 AEs, 2 BDRs, and Sales Operations) to accelerate revenue while building a scalable sales engine for complex, multi-stakeholder deals up to $1M+ ACV. Responsibilities include AE coaching, standardizing BDR processes, partnering with Sales Ops on pipeline health and enablement, and aligning with Marketing as one GTM team. Requires a professional services/SaaS background, strong skills in ICP refinement, value proposition design, pricing strategy, and a data-driven, metrics-first leadership approach.
score_reason: Strong GTM alignment, data-driven metrics focus, and sales operations partnership experience match well; however, this is a pure sales leadership role rather than RevOps/GTM strategy position where Omri typically adds most value, and lacks the operational/systems architecture emphasis that defines his executive profile.
ai_opener: Omri here—your GTM stack caught my eye. I've spent 15+ years architecting revenue engines in B2B SaaS, and I notice Belkins is scaling complex deals with distributed sales teams. While Head of Sales isn't my typical lane (I usually build the RevOps infrastructure behind it), I'm deeply familiar with the Sales Ops partnership, pipeline forecasting, and ICP+pricing strategy that makes teams like yours predictable. Worth a quick call to explore if there's a GTM strategy angle here?
